segments.title: Beaumaris Castle sea access and concentric defense
segments.summary: The Beaumaris lecture presents the castle as a concentric fortress whose moat, dock, town wall, and Menai Strait position turned architecture into strategic control.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke uses Beaumaris to show how castle design could combine intimidation, siege endurance, and maritime supply. The concentric plan, surviving moat, protected dock, and planned town wall made the site difficult to rush and useful for holding Anglesey. Its position near the Menai Strait also tied the castle to observation, reinforcement, and control of movement between North Wales and the island.

evidence.note: The transcript explains the concentric layout, moat, gates, siege-survival role, and dock connected to the sea.
The transcript links the protected dock, town wall, Anglesey geography, and Menai Strait position to strategic control.
